name: Apply pod
description: Accept a K8s manifest via NATS req.spark.apply, parse it (Pod/Job/Deployment/StatefulSet/CronJob), and add pods to the store in Pending state for scheduling

name: Delete pod
description: Delete a pod via NATS req.spark.delete: stop in podman, remove from store, cascade delete from SQLite

name: Get pod status
description: Query a single pod's full status via NATS req.spark.get: name, status, priority, timestamps, restarts, event history

name: List pods
description: List all pods (optionally filtered by status) via NATS req.spark.list: name, status, priority summaries

name: Resource-aware scheduling
description: Scheduler admits pods only when CPU, memory, and GPU resources are available after system reserves. ResourceTracker tracks allocations per pod.

name: Priority preemption
description: Higher-priority pods can preempt lower-priority pods when resources are insufficient. Anti-thrash protection prevents repeated preemption (>3 in 5 min).

name: Pod lifecycle reconciliation
description: 5-second reconciler loop drives pods through pending -> scheduled -> running -> completed/failed. Checks actual podman state for running pods.

name: Restart policy
description: Handles Always (always restart), OnFailure (retry with backoffLimit), and Never restart policies when pods exit

name: CronJob scheduling
description: CronScheduler ticks every 30s, fires registered CronJobs on schedule, supports Allow/Forbid/Replace concurrency policies, prunes job history

name: Directory watcher
description: Polls /etc/spark/manifests every 5s for YAML files. Detects add/modify/remove via SHA256 checksums. Applies pods and registers CronJobs. Persists to SQLite.

name: Heartbeat publishing
description: Publishes node heartbeat to heartbeat.spark.{nodeId} at configurable interval. Includes CPU/RAM/GPU availability, pod counts, uptime.

name: Event publishing
description: Publishes pod lifecycle events to evt.spark.{status}.{podName} on every status change (scheduled, running, completed, failed, preempted)

name: Log streaming
description: Streams pod stdout/stderr to log.spark.{podName} via podman pod logs --follow. Batches lines (max 10, 100ms flush). Starts on Running, stops on exit.

name: GPU detection and resource tracking
description: Detects NVIDIA GPUs via nvidia-smi (model, memory, utilization, count). Detects system CPU/RAM. Feeds into scheduler ResourceTracker.

name: State persistence (SQLite)
description: Persists all pod state to SQLite with WAL mode. Saves on status change, apply, and delete. Loads on startup. Schema: pods table + events table with CASCADE.

name: Pod recovery after restart
description: On startup, loads persisted state from SQLite, queries podman for actual pods, reconciles: updates stale statuses, marks lost pods as Failed, logs orphans.

name: Retention pruning
description: Every 10 minutes, prunes completed/failed pods older than --pod-retention (default 7d) from both in-memory store and SQLite.

name: Apply pod via HTTP
description: POST /api/v1/pods accepts a K8s YAML manifest, parses it, applies pods to the store, persists to SQLite. Returns JSON with pod names and statuses.

name: Delete pod via HTTP
description: DELETE /api/v1/pods/{name} stops the pod in podman, removes from store and SQLite. Returns JSON confirmation.

name: Get pod via HTTP
description: GET /api/v1/pods/{name} returns full pod detail (status, priority, timestamps, restarts, events) as JSON.

name: List pods via HTTP
description: GET /api/v1/pods returns all pods as JSON array. Optional ?status= query parameter for filtering.

name: Health check endpoint
description: GET /healthz returns HTTP 200 with {"status":"ok"} when spark is ready. Used by load balancers and monitoring.

name: Resource usage endpoint
description: GET /api/v1/resources returns current node resource state: allocatable, allocated, available CPU/memory/GPU as JSON.

name: Resource reconciliation
description: Periodically queries podman and nvidia-smi for actual resource usage of running pods. Compares actual vs requested, logs discrepancies, updates tracker.

name: Graceful shutdown
description: On SIGTERM/SIGINT, stops accepting new pods, drains running pods (waits up to --shutdown-timeout), closes HTTP server, SQLite, and NATS cleanly.

name: Prometheus metrics endpoint
description: GET /metrics returns node and pod metrics in Prometheus text exposition format. Includes CPU, memory, GPU utilization, pod counts by status, scheduling and preemption counters, HTTP request totals.

name: HTTP API authentication
description: Bearer token authentication middleware on all HTTP endpoints except /healthz. Rejects unauthenticated requests with 401. Token configured via --api-token-file flag.

name: Pod logs via HTTP
description: GET /api/v1/pods/{name}/logs returns recent pod log output as text/plain. Supports ?tail=N for last N lines and ?follow=true for SSE streaming.

name: Pod events via HTTP
description: GET /api/v1/pods/{name}/events returns lifecycle event history from SQLite as JSON array. Supports ?since= for time filtering.

name: Structured JSON logging
description: Configurable log output format via --log-format flag (text or json). JSON mode uses slog.JSONHandler for machine-parseable log aggregation.

name: EmptyDir volume support
description: EmptyDir volumes create temporary directories that are mounted into containers and cleaned up on pod removal. Mapped to podman tmpfs mounts.
